items.title: 'Her life is ruined': Hellenic Club cleared of failing to record problem gambling

children.content: The Hellenic Club has been cleared of breaches of failing to record the problem gambling of a Canberra couple despite the pair losing at least $345,000 on poker machines.

items.title: 'I was in tears': Living without a heater through Canberra's winter

children.content: Barbara Belmonte, an ACT public housing resident, has been waiting weeks for her heater to be repaired amid the sub-zero temperatures of a Canberra winter — and she is not the only one.

image.altText: Barbara Belmont, public housing resident, sits and gestures while talking.

items.title: New AI-powered alert system to warn drivers of approaching wildlife

children.content: An estimated 10 million animals are killed on Australian roads each year. It's hoped a new artificial intelligence-powered alert system being trialled on the Monaro Highway near Canberra will reduce the toll.

image.altText: A wildlife sign with a digital panel at the bottom.

items.title: Home Affairs asks DV survivors to prove abuser relationship 'genuine'

children.content: A federal government safety net has been identified as one of the biggest stressors for migrant women experiencing domestic and family violence.

gallery.altText: An anonymous woman in the city, back of head

thumbnail.altText: An anonymous woman in the city, back of head

items.title: 'We didn't give up': Afghan women's refugee cricketers celebrate ICC decision

children.content: Five years since fleeing Afghanistan after the Taliban returned to power, the country's women's refugee cricket team has secured a pathway to compete at the international level.

image.altText: Benafsha Hashimi, Afghanistan Women's Cricket Team

items.title: 'I didn't feel safe': Mum reveals concerns before childcare closure

children.content: A Canberra mother had concerns for her daughter's safety at Edge Early Learning in Higgins long before the ACT regulator ordered the centre to close.

image.altText: A mother stands with a serious expression with her two children.

items.title: Canberra United FC finally has a buyer. Who are the new owners?

children.content: The new owners of the national capital's A-League Women side have pointed to the launch of a men's team.

items.title: Family of seven's home left uninhabitable as insurer worsens damage

children.content: A Queensland family is living in an overcrowded caravan and shed after insurance provider Suncorp exacerbated damage in their home, initially caused by a leak.

gallery.altText: Family of seven look at camera sitting inside their garage.
Inside a family home in Queensland, that has been damaged by insurer.

Inside a family home in Queensland, that has been damaged by insurer.

items.title: Judgment day for Rebel Wilson's messy defamation case

children.content: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson will learn today whether she defamed the lead actor on her "fun girl-power" film, in a judgment to be handed down in the Federal Court.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson outside court wearing a green jacket.

items.title: 'Not finished': Trump signals US strikes on Iran's 'Pickaxe Mountain' site

children.content: Donald Trump says the United States is far from finished with Iran, warning its next military target could be an underground nuclear complex.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaking while seated in the White House

items.title: 'We do not want to highlight this yet': AI data centre's FAQ slip-up

children.content: Data centre builder Firmus posted a statement, since removed, indicating its intention to get some of its cooling water from the state-owned irrigation system, adding "but we do not want to highlight this yet".

items.title: Labor conference braces for gambling, gas and AI stoushes

children.content: Labor members and unions are gearing up for fights over gambling, gas and AI at the party's national conference later this week.

items.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say

children.content: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

image.altText: Hamish MacDonald,  Allesandra Galloni, Hugh Marks, Michael Miller, Jane Palfreyman, Matt Stanton.

items.title: Map reveals Australia's 'pharmacy deserts' are not all in the bush

children.content: Australia's "pharmacy deserts" have been mapped for the first time, revealing remote NT communities and outer-city Melbourne have fewer pharmacies per person than some other parts of the country.

gallery.altText: A female pharmacist is selecting a drug from the pharmacy inventory.

thumbnail.altText: A female pharmacist is selecting a drug from the pharmacy inventory.

items.title: Sixteen-year-old in custody suspected of killing mother, injuring little sister

children.content: Police say a teenage boy is suspected to have stabbed his mother to death and seriously injured his 12-year-old sister in an alleged family violence homicide in Perth's south-east.

image.altText: Two police forensic officers and a detective in a suit stand behind police tape near two police vehicles on a suburban street.

items.title: Players and fans made the World Cup a success, but the politics shouldn't be forgotten

children.content: The biggest ever World Cup brought diverse communities together and galvanised its non-traditional footballing co-hosts, but the concerns raised from the start have not disappeared in the positive afterglow.

image.altText: A composite image showing USA player Falorin Balogun, Spain's Ferran Torres, and Norway fans

items.title: New Ozploitation horror is like Puberty Blues from hell

children.content: After spending years in special effects and make-up, filmmaker Mia'Kate Russell's ocker nightmare Penny Lane Is Dead has made it to the silver screen.

items.title: Stingers hitting new heights thanks to shared love for each other

children.content: Australia's women's water polo team, the Stingers, were the most successful Australian team at the Paris Olympics, winning a stunning silver medal. This is the secret to their success.

items.title: Victorian teachers are going on strike again — here's what you need to know

children.content: Victorian state school teachers and educators are going ahead with their second 24-hour strike this year after union members rejected the government's latest pay offer.

image.altText: A dense crowd of people in red shirts with some holding signs walk through a CBD street.

items.title: Repatriation effort for homeless man found dead outside Sydney station

children.content: The mother of a homeless man whose death outside a Sydney train station went unnoticed for days is pleading for her son's remains to be returned to Nepal.

image.altText: Composite of Bikram and his mother Seti

items.title: Iran war delays upgrades to Queensland’s Triple Zero phone system

children.content: The Iran war has been a contributing factor to Queensland's Triple Zero phone system being delayed until next year, the ABC understands.

items.title: 'Irresponsible': Russia conducts live-fire weapons exercise off British coast

children.content: A UK minister has confirmed that a Russian naval vessel conducted a "live-fire weapons exercise" in international waters off the coast of Plymouth in south-west England.

image.altText: A warship at sea

items.title: Falling 200kg drive gear injures steel worker months after workplace death

children.content: There have been six incidents involving falling tools and building materials at BlueScope, less than a year since contractor Jack McGrath was killed.

image.altText: Worker looking out on industrial plant.

items.title: Whyalla workers injured at steelworks' blast furnace

children.content: Three Whyalla steelworks employees have been treated for injuries suffered during attempts to restart the site's troubled blast furnace, the ABC understands.

items.title: Who paid for Pauline Hanson's trip to Europe? Not taxpayers, she says

children.content: In her first appearance since returning to Australia, Pauline Hanson fields questions about her trip to Europe with billionaire Gina Rinehart.

items.title: Police confirm investigation involving incident with NT administrator

children.content: Former army commando turned Labor MP, Luke Gosling, has denied involvement in an alleged incident with King Charles's representative in the NT.

items.title: UK politician Ann Widdecombe hit 21 times with hammer, prosecutors allege

children.content: Joshua Kerry, 28, appears in the Westminster Magistrates' Court charged with murder over a killing that has shocked the public and political establishment.

items.title: Job system broken for workers, booming for private equity, report says

children.content: A new report argues Australia's employment services system is failing jobseekers, while filling the pockets of private equity firms and millionaires.

image.altText: people queue up outside a Centrelink office

items.title: Rain cancels outback reunions and events for second year

children.content: Flood-damaged roads and soggy racetracks mean some of the outback's favourite events are being cancelled and keeping tourists away.
